roadmap.notes: Lands GitHub #170 (supersedes #169 settle-window primitive). Six ADRs: 0054 (ProbeRunner subsystem), 0055 (ServiceLifecycleReconciler), 0056 (ServiceSubmitEvent V2 wire evolution — single-cut delete ConvergedRunning/ConvergedFailed, add Stable/Failed), 0057 ([[health_check.*]] TOML spec), 0058 (default TCP startup probe inference — honest-by-default), 0059 (exec-probe cgroup placement). Object-oriented Rust paradigm per CLAUDE.md; @nw-software-crafter for implementation. The eight slice scaffolds in docs/feature/service-health-check-probes/slices/ are the decomposition starting point. Slices 01+08 collapse into phase 01 (walking-skeleton + EarlyExit are structurally one composition root flow per slice-08 §Importance); slice 04 (readiness) co-lands with slice 07 (kind rejection) in step 03-01 because slice 07 is a small parallel parser change; slices 02/03/05/06 each own their own step. Reconciler shape per ADR-0035/0036: pure sync reconcile, runtime-owned ViewStore, View persists inputs (consecutive_failures, consecutive_successes, stable_announced set, startup_attempts) not derived deadlines per .claude/rules/development.md § "Persist inputs, not derived state". rkyv envelope `ProbeResultRowEnvelope::V1` per ADR-0048 with discriminant pinning (`const FIXTURE_V1_DISCRIMINANT: u8 = 0;`) per QR1 callout in feature-delta.md. ServiceSubmitEvent wire is single-cut greenfield migration per CLAUDE.md § feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md (no parallel-fields period, delete ConvergedRunning/ConvergedFailed in same diff as Stable/Failed). Mutation gate ≥80% kill rate per `.claude/rules/testing.md`; all Tier 3 tests gated behind `integration-tests` feature and run via `cargo xtask lima run --` per Lima rule. Effort estimates advisory per CLAUDE.md. Original walking-skeleton step (01-03) re-sliced 2026-05-25 — reconciler-core landed in commit 2fabf259, subsequent work split across follow-on atomic sub-steps (01-03b/c/d/e/f) per checkpoint-commit signal; total_steps 8 → 13.

phases.name: Walking skeleton — foundation types + default TCP startup probe + ServiceLifecycle plumbing
phases.description: Establishes the structural foundation every later slice composes onto, then lands the end-to-end coinflip-as-Service regression guard for RCA-A. Three steps split foundational types (port traits, ProbeResultRow envelope, ProbeDescriptor / ProbeMechanic / ProbeRole / ProbeIdx aggregate, ServiceFailureReason + ProbeWitness + ServiceLifecycleState + ServiceLifecycleView in overdrive-core) from TOML parser extension (ServiceSpec V1→V2 envelope per ADR-0048 procedure, default TCP startup inference per ADR-0058) from the reconciler + wire + ProbeRunner subsystem + walking-skeleton integration test. Slice 08's EarlyExit detection branch is co-located in step 01-03 because the EarlyExit code path is the ServiceLifecycleReconciler's other RCA-A-closing branch (the coinflip-as-Service 100-seed regression test naturally exercises both StartupProbeFailed and EarlyExit). Single-cut wire migration per CLAUDE.md § feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md — `ServiceSubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` / `ConvergedFailed` are deleted in the same diff that adds `Stable` / `Failed { reason: ServiceFailureReason }`.

steps.name: Foundation types — Prober trait surface + ProbeResultRowEnvelope V1 + ProbeDescriptor aggregate + ServiceLifecycleState/View + TerminalCondition::Stable/Failed variants + workspace deps
steps.description:
Lands the typed foundation every later step composes onto. CREATE NEW: crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/prober.rs declares three port traits (`TcpProber`, `HttpProber`, `ExecProber`) with FULL rustdoc per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature" — preconditions, postconditions, edge cases, observable invariants (cgroup-placement-failure surface mapping to `ProbeFailure::ExecSpawnFailed { reason }` per ADR-0054 QR2). CREATE NEW: crates/overdrive-core/src/observation/probe_result_row.rs with `ProbeResultRow` payload + `ProbeResultRowEnvelope::V1(ProbeResultRowV1)` rkyv envelope per ADR-0048 procedure (alias-to-payload, `pub type ProbeResultRow = ProbeResultRowV1`, codec-internal envelope not re-exported from lib.rs, golden-bytes fixture in crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/probe_result_row.rs pinning `const FIXTURE_V1_DISCRIMINANT: u8 = 0;` per ADR-0054 §5 QR1). CREATE NEW: crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/probe_descriptor.rs with `ProbeDescriptor`, `ProbeMechanic { Tcp { host, port } | Http { path, port, host } | Exec { command, args } }`, `ProbeRole { Startup | Readiness | Liveness }`, `ProbeIdx` newtype with `FromStr` / `Display` / serde per § Newtype completeness. CREATE NEW: crates/overdrive-core/src/service_lifecycle.rs with `ServiceFailureReason { StartupProbeFailed { probe_idx, last_fail, attempts } | EarlyExit { exit_code } | BackoffExhausted { attempts } }` (#[non_exhaustive], #[from] for ProbeFailure) + `ProbeWitness { probe_idx, role, mechanic_summary, inferred }` + `ServiceLifecycleState` + `ServiceLifecycleView` (typed View — Serialize+Deserialize+Default+Clone+Send+Sync per ADR-0035; carries `stable_announced: BTreeSet<AllocationId>`, `consecutive_failures: BTreeMap<(AllocationId, ProbeIdx), u32>`, `consecutive_successes: BTreeMap<(AllocationId, ProbeIdx), u32>`, `startup_attempts: BTreeMap<(AllocationId, ProbeIdx), u32>`; ALL inputs, NEVER derived deadlines). EXTEND: crates/overdrive-core/src/transition_reason.rs adds `TerminalCondition::Stable { settled_in: Duration, witness: ProbeWitness }` and `TerminalCondition::Failed { reason: ServiceFailureReason }` per ADR-0037 §5 additive minor SemVer (#[non_exhaustive] preserved). EXTEND: workspace Cargo.toml promotes `hyper-util` + `tokio-util` from transitive to direct workspace deps with `.workspace = true` consumers (used in later steps); workspace member declares `integration-tests = []` per § Workspace convention. Sim adapters scaffold: CREATE NEW crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/probers.rs with `SimTcpProber` / `SimHttpProber` / `SimExecProber` queue-driven (`enqueue_outcome(probe_idx, outcome)`) — `Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<ProbeOutcome>>>` per-prober, no auto-progress per § "Production code is not shaped by simulation" (production probers do not get sim-shape concessions). EXTEND xtask::dst_lint AST scanner: new clause scans `overdrive-worker::probe_runner::ProbeRunner` impl blocks (or its eventual location) for a declared `probe(&self) -> Result<(), ProbeError>` method per ADR-0054 §7 Earned-Trust; this is the third-layer structural enforcement per `.claude/rules/development.md` principle 12.

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory, Rust-translated): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests via proptest! {} blocks with explicit Strategy generators + invariant assertions over a declared state universe (the proptest equivalent of state-delta matchers). Single-example #[test] is FALLBACK only — document why in a `// bypass:` comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as proptest invariants (quantified preconditions + invariant outcomes) where the contract supports it; classic single-case #[tokio::test] is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example (under the integration-tests feature gate per .claude/rules/testing.md) — they verify wiring, not invariants. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked.

STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero). No legacy bug-prone code in scope this step.
steps.criteria: All foundation types declared with full rustdoc + derives; ProbeResultRowEnvelope V1 round-trips bit-identically; golden-bytes fixture pins V1 discriminant byte; all newtypes (ProbeIdx) round-trip Display/FromStr/serde; SimTcpProber/SimHttpProber/SimExecProber implement their trait surface returning queue-driven outcomes; xtask::dst_lint Earned-Trust scanner declared (firing once probe_runner module lands in 01-03); workspace deps land; cargo check --workspace --all-features passes; trybuild compile-pass fixture proves Box<dyn TcpProber> dyn-compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: S-SHCP-ENV-01 → proptest `ProbeResultRowEnvelopeRoundtrip` over `arbitrary::<ProbeResultRowV1>()` — for every generated row, `archive_for_store` → `from_store_bytes` → byte-equal AND `into_latest()` returns Ok(value) PartialEq-equal to original. Universe = full ProbeResultRowV1 product type (alloc_id × probe_idx × role × status × last_observed_at × last_fail_reason). Lands at crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/probe_result_row_envelope.rs

S-SHCP-ENV-02 → schema-evolution fixture at crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/probe_result_row.rs declares `const FIXTURE_V1: &str = "..."` (hex-encoded archived bytes of a canonical ProbeResultRowV1) AND `const FIXTURE_V1_DISCRIMINANT: u8 = 0;`. Test hex-decodes FIXTURE_V1, asserts byte[0] == FIXTURE_V1_DISCRIMINANT, rkyv-deserialises into ProbeResultRowEnvelope, calls into_latest(), asserts equality against canonical Latest projection. Per ADR-0054 §5 QR1 + `.claude/rules/testing.md` § Archive schema-evolution roundtrip.

S-SHCP-ENV-03 → behavioural test: unknown envelope variant (synthesise byte[0] = 0xff) deserialises into `EnvelopeError::UnknownVariant`; ObservationStore consumer logs + skips (warn-not-fail-fast) per ADR-0048 § Unknown / malformed handling is asymmetric by layer (observation gossips, converges).

Three port traits declared in crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/prober.rs with rustdoc preconditions (`TcpProber::probe` — host must be parseable IP, port > 0; `HttpProber::probe` — URL must be plain HTTP per Phase 1; `ExecProber::probe` — command must be non-empty, cgroup_path must exist), postconditions (Pass = TCP connected within timeout / 2xx within timeout / exit code 0 within timeout), edge cases (HTTP 3xx → Fail with Redirect{code} per US-02 / research § 6.1 Pitfall 5; exec command-not-found → Fail with ExecSpawnFailed{reason}; cgroup-placement EACCES/ENOSPC → ExecSpawnFailed{reason} per ADR-0054 QR2 (NOT auto-retried)), observable invariants (probe outcome is deterministic for fixed inputs against the same backing system; no side effect on caller besides the returned ProbeOutcome).

Newtype `ProbeIdx(u8)` per § Newtype completeness — FromStr returns Result<Self, ParseError> with case-insensitive accept; Display canonical decimal form; serde matches Display/FromStr; proptest `ProbeIdxRoundtrip` for every valid u8 0..=255 round-trips.

TerminalCondition gains two new variants behind #[non_exhaustive]: a Stable variant observably carries the elapsed duration from alloc start to the first startup-probe Pass observation, plus a witness identifier sufficient to attribute the Pass (probe index, role, mechanic-kind summary, whether inferred); a Failed variant observably carries a typed reason discriminated by failure class (StartupTimeout, StartupProbeFailed, EarlyExit, LivenessProbeFailed). Existing variants unchanged; rkyv archive layout pinned via schema_evolution fixture (additive variant — V<N>Envelope bump deferred to whichever step first lands a breaking shape change; this step's additive append is binary-compatible per ADR-0048 § "Why a per-type rkyv enum is forward-compatible across variant additions").

SimTcpProber / SimHttpProber / SimExecProber implement their trait surface; queue-driven via `Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<ProbeOutcome>>>` (BTreeMap-keyed where iteration matters; HashMap forbidden without dst-lint marker); proptest `SimProberFifoIsObserved` — for arbitrary outcome sequences, dequeue order equals enqueue order.

steps.name: ServiceLifecycleReconciler core reconcile body — Stable / StartupProbeFailed / EarlyExit branches (reconciler-core checkpoint)
steps.description:
LANDED 2026-05-24 in commit 2fabf259. Lands the pure-sync ServiceLifecycleReconciler core reconcile body per ADR-0035 collapsed Reconciler trait: `fn reconcile(&self, desired: &ServiceLifecycleState, actual: &ServiceLifecycleState, view: &ServiceLifecycleView, tick: &TickContext) -> (Vec<Action>, ServiceLifecycleView)`; NO .await, NO port dependencies, NO wall-clock outside `tick.now`. Reconcile branches (a/b/c) and idempotent-no-op branch (d) all in scope: (a) AllocStatusRow.state == Running AND any startup-probe Pass row exists AND alloc NOT in view.stable_announced → emit Stable terminal-condition action + insert into next-View.stable_announced; (b) startup_attempts ≥ max_attempts AND no Pass row AND startup_deadline elapsed → emit Failed { StartupProbeFailed }; (c) AllocStatusRow.state == Failed AND elapsed_since_started_at < startup_deadline AND no Pass row → emit Failed { EarlyExit } (Slice 08 RCA-A regression branch); (d) all other inputs → no actions, no view mutation. Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Persist inputs, not derived state" — the View carries inputs only (stable_announced set, per-alloc-per-probe counters); deadlines recomputed every tick. SCOPE LIMITED to this step: reconcile-decision logic + 6 acceptance tests (S-SHCP-RECON-01..06) + ServiceAllocFact + ServiceLifecycleState (concrete struct). DEFERRED to follow-on sub-steps: AnyReconciler dispatch wiring (→ 01-03b), ProbeRunner subsystem + observation-store probe-row writes + Earned-Trust dst_lint scanner clause (→ 01-03c), ExecDriver lifecycle hooks + AllocationSpec.probe_descriptors + composition-root Earned-Trust gate (→ 01-03d), ServiceSubmitEvent V1→V2 single-cut wire migration (→ 01-03e), Tier-3 walking-skeleton CLI test + K1 coinflip-100-seed regression (→ 01-03f). Original walking-skeleton step re-sliced 2026-05-25 — reconciler-core landed in commit 2fabf259; subsequent work spread across follow-on sub-steps per CLAUDE.md § "No effort/time budget cuts" (a checkpoint commit against a multi-session deliverable is the structural signal to re-slice rather than fold).

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory, Rust-translated): Unit tests MUST be proptest! {} blocks with explicit Strategy generators + invariant assertions; single-example #[test] is FALLBACK only. Acceptance scenarios framed as proptest invariants where the contract supports it.

STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero).
steps.criteria: ServiceLifecycleReconciler reconcile is pure sync (NO .await, NO port deps, NO wall-clock outside tick.now); branches (a) Stable, (b) StartupProbeFailed, (c) EarlyExit, (d) idempotent-no-op all land; ServiceAllocFact + concrete ServiceLifecycleState struct land; 6 acceptance tests at service_lifecycle_stable.rs (S-SHCP-RECON-01..06) pass. AnyReconciler dispatch + ProbeRunner subsystem + wire migration + walking-skeleton DEFERRED to 01-03b/c/d/e/f.
acceptance_criteria: S-SHCP-RECON-01 — proptest `StableEmittedOnRunningWithPassProbe` — universe = arbitrary (AllocStatusRow.state ∈ {Pending,Running,Failed}) × (probe Pass-rows present 0..3) × (alloc in stable_announced set Y/N) × (elapsed since started_at 0..3600s). For every state where state==Running AND any startup-probe Pass row exists AND alloc NOT in stable_announced: reconcile emits exactly ONE Action::SetTerminalCondition { Stable { settled_in: elapsed_to_first_pass, witness } } AND next-View.stable_announced contains the alloc. Lands at crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/service_lifecycle_stable.rs

S-SHCP-RECON-02 — proptest `StableDedupedOnceAnnounced` — for arbitrary inputs identical to S-SHCP-RECON-01 case EXCEPT alloc IS in stable_announced: reconcile emits ZERO actions AND next-View == view (idempotent).

S-SHCP-RECON-03 — proptest `StartupProbeFailedEmittedAfterExhaustion` — for arbitrary view.startup_attempts ≥ max_attempts AND no Pass row exists AND elapsed ≥ startup_deadline: reconcile emits exactly ONE Action::SetTerminalCondition { Failed { reason: StartupProbeFailed { probe_idx, last_fail, attempts: <observed counter> } } }.

S-SHCP-RECON-04 — proptest `EarlyExitDetectedBeforeAnyPass` (the RCA-A close) — universe = arbitrary AllocStatusRow.state == Failed × exit_code 0..255 × elapsed_since_started 0..startup_deadline × NO Pass row exists. For every such tuple: reconcile emits Action::SetTerminalCondition { Failed { reason: EarlyExit { exit_code } } }. NOTE: the wire-event projection clause (ServiceSubmitEvent::Failed { reason: EarlyExit, stderr_tail }) is DEFERRED to sub-step 01-03e (wire migration).

S-SHCP-PURITY-01 — compile-time witness: ServiceLifecycleReconciler::reconcile is callable from a non-async non-tokio context (sync `fn`); the signature has no `&mut self`, no `.await`, no `&dyn Clock`/`&dyn Transport`/`&dyn ObservationStore` parameter. Lands at crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/service_lifecycle_stable.rs (PURITY-02 view-purity assertion is DEFERRED to 01-03b).
implementation_scope: crates/overdrive-core/src/service_lifecycle.rs [LANDED — ServiceAllocFact + ServiceLifecycleState concrete struct + ServiceLifecycleReconciler impl Reconciler with reconcile branches (a) Stable, (b) StartupProbeFailed, (c) EarlyExit, (d) idempotent-no-op]

steps.description:

Lands S-SHCP-PURITY-02 (View carries no derived-state slots) as a proptest / rg-verifiable structural assertion. This step is small and explicitly the unblocker for every other sub-step — every downstream change composes onto AnyReconciler.

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory, Rust-translated): Unit tests MUST be proptest! {} blocks with explicit Strategy generators + invariant assertions; single-example #[test] is FALLBACK only.

STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero). reconcilers/mod.rs extension is Stage 1 first (state-delta of the new enum variants across existing match arms) then Stage 2 PBT amplification.
steps.criteria: AnyState/AnyReconcilerView/AnyReconciler dispatch enums extended with ServiceLifecycle variants; cross-crate match arms exhaustive (cargo check --workspace --all-features passes); service_lifecycle_purity.rs lands S-SHCP-PURITY-02 (View carries inputs only — no `next_*_at` / `*_deadline` / `is_stable`-shaped field name, rg-verifiable); existing acceptance tests in service_lifecycle_stable.rs continue to pass.
acceptance_criteria: S-SHCP-PURITY-02 — ServiceLifecycleView carries no derived-state slots: no boolean recording "alloc has been announced Stable" expressed as cached outcome, no `next_*_at`-shaped deadline computed at write time (rg verifiable for `next_*_at`, `*_deadline`, `is_stable`-shaped identifiers as field names). The View carries only inputs — semantically: (i) a per-allocation Stable-announcement record sufficient to make Stable emission idempotent (a once-only flag per alloc), (ii) per-allocation-per-probe consecutive-failure counters, (iii) per-allocation-per-probe consecutive-success counters, and (iv) per-allocation-per-probe startup-attempt counters. Container choice (BTreeMap vs HashMap-with-marker) is the crafter's per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Ordered-collection choice" — observed iteration ⇒ BTreeMap, point-access only ⇒ HashMap with `// dst-lint: hashmap-ok <reason>`; the AC does not prescribe.
